input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import csv, collections
with open('market.csv', newline='') as f: rows=list(csv.DictReader(f))
active=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved']=='False']
resolved=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved']=='True']
print('rows',len(rows),'active',len(active),'resolved',len(resolved))
print('active without prediction', sum(1 for r in active if not r['my_prediction']))
print('resolved with gt', sum(1 for r in resolved if r['ground_truth']))
print('active pred dates')
for k,v in collections.Counter(r['my_prediction_date'] or 'None' for r in active).most_common(25): print(k,v)
print('\\nlast 25 resolved rows:')
for r in resolved[-25:]:
    print(r['qid'], '| gt=', r['ground_truth'], '| pred_date=', r['my_prediction_date'], '| title=', r['title'][:140].replace('\\n',' '), '| pred=', r['my_prediction'][:160])
print('\\nactive resolving <= Feb02:')
for r in active:
    if r['resolution_date'] <= '2026-02-02':
        print(r['resolution_date'], r['qid'], r['my_prediction_date'], r['title'][:130].replace('\\n',' '), 'P=', r['my_prediction'][:180])
PY"

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import csv, collections
with open('market.csv', newline='') as f: rows=list(csv.DictReader(f))
active=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved']=='False']
resolved=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved']=='True']
print('rows',len(rows),'active',len(active),'resolved',len(resolved))
print('active without prediction', sum(1 for r in active if not r['my_prediction']))
print('resolved with gt', sum(1 for r in resolved if r['ground_truth']))
print('active pred dates')
for k,v in collections.Counter(r['my_prediction_date'] or 'None' for r in active).most_common(25): print(k,v)
print('\\nlast 25 resolved rows:')
for r in resolved[-25:]:
    print(r['qid'], '| gt=', r['ground_truth'], '| pred_date=', r['my_prediction_date'], '| title=', r['title'][:140].replace('\\n',' '), '| pred=', r['my_prediction'][:160])
print('\\nactive resolving <= Feb02:')
for r in active:
    if r['resolution_date'] <= '2026-02-02':
        print(r['resolution_date'], r['qid'], r['my_prediction_date'], r['title'][:130].replace('\\n',' '), 'P=', r['my_prediction'][:180])
PY"

blocks.text: Jan 24 resolved three more sports questions: Pegula, Iva Jovic, and Sam Darnold, all covered by the recent updates. I’m focusing today’s scan on the Jan 25-29 event-location and politics markets, plus Australian Open title and upcoming draw questions.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json
cut='2026-01-24'; paths=['articles/2026/01/24/articles.jsonl']
queries={
 'q110125_indigenous':['Indigenous rights','Australia','explosive','January 26','protest','Melbourne','Sydney'],
 'q238071_sudan_siege':['Sudan','siege','broken','state','North Kordofan','North Darfur'],
 'q267761_tariff':['15%','25%','tariff','Trump','Denmark','France','Greenland'],
 'q320820_gaza_children':['24 children','Gaza','WHO','World Health Organization','evacuated'],
 'q949_greece_explosion':['Greek','Greece','factory explosion','workers','killed'],
 'q976773_iaea':['UN nuclear watchdog','IAEA','Ukraine','special meeting','requested'],
 'q988168_bisan':['Bisan Owda','permanently banned','Instagram','Facebook','TikTok','X'],
 'q465445_fury':['Tyson Fury','April 11','comeback','opponent','Makhmudov','Wardley','Brandon Moore'],
 'q910937_aus_leader_threats':['Australia','online threats','charged','foreign leader','visit'],
 'q430936_south_sudan':['South Sudan','235,000','displaced','Upper Nile','UN'],
 'q789988_netanyahu_visit':['Netanyahu','Somaliland','visit Israel','Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi'],
 'q956334_wafrica_parties':['political parties dissolved','West African','Mali','Burkina Faso','Niger','Guinea'],
 'q176447_epstein_commerce':['Epstein','Commerce Secretary','island','Wilbur Ross','Howard Lutnick'],
 'q1877_ski_crash':['Crans-Montana','World Cup downhill','airlifted','hospital','Breezy Johnson','Lindsey Vonn'],
 'q562701_balochistan':['Balochistan','three-day battle','Pakistani police','secured','town'],
 'q261538_cl_newcastle':['Champions League playoff','Newcastle','first leg','city','draw'],
 'q55999_cl_benfica':['Champions League playoff','Benfica','first leg','Real Madrid','Juventus'],
 'q35131_cl_juventus':['Champions League playoff','Juventus','eliminate','Real Madrid','Benfica'],
 'q85609_cl_realmadrid':['Champions League playoff','Real Madrid','draw','opponent','Benfica'],
 'q316535_cummins':['Pat Cummins','replacement','T20 World Cup squad','Sean Abbott','Spencer Johnson'],
 'q951896_ao_men_title':['Australian Open','Jannik Sinner','Carlos Alcaraz','Novak Djokovic','men\\'s singles'],
 'q946810_grammy':['Album of the Year','Grammy','Kendrick Lamar','Lady Gaga','Bad Bunny'],
 'q391811_ukraine_bus':['drone attack','bus carrying miners','Ukraine','miners','city'],
 'q492629_harden':['James Harden','Clippers','trade','receive'],
}
for name, words in queries.items():
    hits=[]
    for path in paths:
        for line in open(path, errors='ignore'):
            try:a=json.loads(line)
            except: continue
            dp=(a.get('date_publish') or '')[:10]
            if dp and dp>cut: continue
            text=((a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or ''))
            low=text.lower(); score=sum(1 for w in words if w.lower() in low)
            if score>=max(2,min(4,len(words))): hits.append((score,dp,a.get('source'),a.get('title'),a.get('url'),text))
    hits=sorted(hits,key=lambda x:(-x[0],x[1]))[:6]
    print('\\n##',name,'hits',len(hits))
    for score,dp,src,title,url,text in hits:
        print(score,dp,src,title)
        print(' ',url)
        low=text.lower(); idx=min([low.find(w.lower()) for w in words if low.find(w.lower())>=0] or [0])
        print(' ',text[max(0,idx-180):idx+620].replace('\\n',' '))
PY"

The 24-time major winner became the first player to reach 400 wins in Grand Slam singles when he beat Botic van de Zandschulp 6-3, 6-4, 7-6 (4) on Saturday night in the third round of the Australian Open. It improved his win-loss record to 102-10 at the Australian Open, too, equaling Roger Federer's career haul for the most-ever match wins at the season's first major. ### Tyson Fury says facing heavyweight champion Fabio Wardley is a 'good option' if Anthony Joshua fight doesn't happen https://www.skysports.com/boxing/news/12040/13498284/tyson-fury-says-facing-heavyweight-champion-fabio-wardley-is-a-good-option-if-anthony-joshua-fight-doesnt-happen 2026-01-24

-- Fabio Wardley --
Tyson Fury says a fight with heavyweight world champion Fabio Wardley would be a "good option" for him later in 2026 as he plots his return to action. Having announced his return from a latest 'retirement' that has seen him remain inactive since a December 2024 defeat to Oleksandr Usyk, former champion Fury is expected to return to action in April. It appears Fury's return will come against a lower-level opponent before he seeks a bigger bout later in the year. That second fight had long been expected to come against Anthony Joshua, but the latter's boxing future is unclear after he was a passenger in a December car crash in Nigeria in which two of his close friends were killed. Who is the best option for Fury's comeback fight? Latest boxing headlines, reports and highlights Not got Sky? Fury wrote: "Let's see how I am looking in my return fight, then later in 2026 this [Wardley] would be a good op

plots his return to action. Fury wrote: "Let's see how I am looking in my return fight, then later in 2026 this [Wardley] would be a good option for me to try become 3 x heavyweight champion. "@fabiowardley has a hard fight himself coming up soon." Wardley became WBO interim champion by claiming a huge upset win over Joseph Parker in October, and was then elevated to full champion status when Usyk gave up his undisputed status by vacating the title. Jarrell Miller The controversial American failed drug tests when he was supposed to box Anthony Joshua. But after returning to the sport he has established himself as an international heavyweight gatekeeper. A big man, he gave Daniel Dubois a solid workout before eventually losing by stoppage. In his most recent bout Miller actually drew with Andy Ruiz over 12 rounds. Agron Smakici The 6ft 6in Croatian southpaw is not well known in the sport, but has amassed a 21-3 record, with 19 of those wins coming by knockout. And he does know Fury, the two have been sparring partners and there is potentially some ill-feeling between them - they were sparring when Fury picked up the cut that delayed his first fight with Usyk. Justis Huni The young Australian would be a point of comparison between Fury and new world champion Wardley.
